title: Epstein Sighting Watch
subtitle: Official status: deceased. Internet sighting claims: unverified.
officialBoundary: The official record treats Jeffrey Epstein as deceased as of August 10, 2019. This watch does not claim he is alive. It tracks internet sightings, lookalike claims, fake-media claims, memes, and public speculation as claims only.
rules: Official status remains deceased unless a public authority or primary record changes it.
A lookalike, screenshot, anonymous post, or viral video is not proof of identity.
Do not name, harass, dox, or accuse ordinary people who resemble Epstein.
Do not publish private addresses, private family details, or unverified personal identifiers.
Every item must be labelled as claim, sighting, lookalike, fake-media risk, debunk, or public-record relevance.
The ledger ranks source strength; it does not declare survival.

evidenceLevels.label: Screenshot repost
evidenceLevels.meaning: Reposted claim with unclear origin or context.

evidenceLevels.label: Photo / video claim
evidenceLevels.meaning: Image or clip exists, but identity/geolocation/source chain not established.

evidenceLevels.label: Geolocated media claim
evidenceLevels.meaning: Image or clip has a location/date argument, still not proof of identity.

evidenceLevels.label: Named witness claim
evidenceLevels.meaning: A named person makes the claim; still needs corroboration.

evidenceLevels.label: Multiple independent sources
evidenceLevels.meaning: Multiple independent sources report the same claim; still not official proof.

evidenceLevels.label: Official/public-record relevance
evidenceLevels.meaning: A public authority, court record, or primary source creates a verifiable contradiction or update.

seedClaims.title: Official status boundary
seedClaims.summary: The ledger begins from the official public record: Epstein is treated as deceased. Sightings are claim traffic unless primary records change the status.
seedClaims.likelyExplanation: Official death record plus public distrust after custody failures creates ongoing survival rumours.

seedClaims.title: Lookalike sighting claim class
seedClaims.summary: Most viral sightings appear to be lookalike/photo claims without identity chain, geolocation, source custody, or official confirmation.
seedClaims.likelyExplanation: Mistaken identity, visual similarity, cropped media, AI enhancement, or hoax framing.

seedClaims.title: AI / manipulated media claim class
seedClaims.summary: Image/video sightings require extra caution because AI, edits, recycled photos, and caption swaps can create false recognition loops.
seedClaims.likelyExplanation: Synthetic image, altered image, old footage, compression artefact, or repost without origin.
